session.user:
# /erk:objective-plan
Create an implementation plan for a specific node in an objective's dependency graph.
## Usage
```bash /erk:objective-plan 3679 /erk:objective-plan 3679 --node 2.1 /erk:objective-plan https://github.com/owner/repo/issues/3679 /erk:objective-plan # prompts for objective reference ```
---
## Agent Instructions
### Step 0: Check for Known Node (Fast Path)
Parse `9272` for `--node <node-id>`. If `--node` is present along with an issue number:
**Invoke the inner skill immediately** via the Skill tool:
``` /erk:system:objective-plan-node <objective-number> --node <node-id> ```
This skips the interactive selection flow (Steps 1-4 below) since the node is already known. The inner skill handles marker creation, marking as planning, context gathering, plan mode, and saving.
**STOP here** — do not proceed to the steps below.
### Step 1: Resolve Objective Reference
Strip `--node` from `9272` if present, then run:
```bash erk exec resolve-objective-ref 9272 ```
**Interpret the JSON result:**
- If `"resolved": true` with `"source": "branch_name"` or `"source": "plan_metadata"`: Inform user "Using objective #N from {source}. Run with explicit argument to override." - If `"resolved": true` with `"source": "argument"`: Use the `objective_number` directly. - If `"resolved": false`: Use AskUserQuestion to ask "What objective issue should I work from?"
### Step 2: Fetch and Set Up Objective Context
Run:
```bash erk exec objective-plan-setup <objective-number> --session-id "157bafef-00db-4abb-9d6e-c9a1f9cf51a2" ```
**Interpret the JSON result:**
- If `"success": false` with `"error": "not_found"`: Report error and exit. - If `"success": false` with `"error": "is_plan"`: Redirect to `/erk:plan-implement`. - If `"success": false` with `"error": "validation_error"`: Report error and exit. - If `"success": true`: Continue with the roadmap data.
Display any `"warnings"` to the user.
### Step 3: Load Objective Skill
Load the `objective` skill for format templates and guidance.
### Step 4: Display Roadmap and Prompt User
Display the roadmap table from the `roadmap.phases` data in the JSON output.
Then use AskUserQuestion to ask which node to plan:
``` Which node should I create a plan for? - Node 1A.1: <description> (Recommended) ← first pending node without plan in progress - Node 1A.2: <description> - Node 2B.1: <description> (plan in progress, #456) ← shown but not recommended - (Other - specify node number or description) ```
**Filtering rules (based on node `status` field from phases):**
- **Show as options:** Nodes with status `"pending"` - **Show but deprioritize:** Nodes with status `"in_progress"` - still selectable via "Other" but not recommended - **Hide from options:** Nodes with status `"done"`, `"blocked"`, or `"skipped"`
**Recommendation rule:** Use `roadmap.next_node` from the JSON as the recommended option. If `next_node` is null, no node is recommended.
If all nodes are complete or have plans in progress, report appropriately:
- All complete: "All roadmap nodes are complete! Consider closing the objective." - All have plans: "All pending nodes have plans in progress. You can still select one via 'Other' to create a parallel plan."
### Step 5: Invoke Inner Skill
After the user selects a node, invoke the inner skill via the Skill tool:
``` /erk:system:objective-plan-node <objective-number> --node <selected-node-id> ```
The inner skill handles marker creation, marking as planning, context gathering, plan mode, and saving. **STOP here** — the inner skill takes over.
---
## Output Format
- **Start:** "Loading objective #<number>..." - **After parsing:** Display roadmap nodes with status - **After selection:** "Creating plan for node <step-id>: <description>" - **In plan mode:** Show plan content - **End:** Always proceed with `/erk:plan-save` — do not offer direct implementation
---
## Error Cases
| Scenario | Action | | --------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------- | | Objective not found | Report error and exit | | Reference is erk-plan | Redirect to `/erk:plan-implement` | | No pending nodes | Report all nodes complete, suggest closing | | Invalid argument format | Prompt for valid issue number | | Roadmap not parseable | Ask user to specify which node to plan | | Verification fails (no objective_issue) | `/erk:plan-save` handles automatically; follow remediation steps |
---
## Important Notes
- **Objective context matters:** Read the full objective for design decisions and lessons learned - **One node at a time:** Each plan should focus on a single roadmap node - **Link back:** Always reference the parent objective in the plan - **Steelthread pattern:** If planning a Phase A node, focus on minimal vertical slice
## Post-Plan Behavior
**CRITICAL: Objective plans must ALWAYS be saved as PRs.** When the exit-plan-mode hook presents save-or-implement options, ALWAYS proceed with "Create a plan PR" by running `/erk:plan-save`. Do NOT offer to implement directly — objective node plans require PR tracking for the objective's roadmap status to work correctly.

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Fetch and set up objective context for #9272

session.tool_result: {"success": true, "objective": {"number": 9272, "title": "Objective: Extract Slot System into Plugin Package", "state": "OPEN", "labels": ["erk-objective"]}, "roadmap": {"phases": [{"number": 1, "suffix": "", "name": "Phase 1", "nodes": [{"id": "1.1", "slug": "create-slots-package", "description": "Create packages/erk-slots/ workspace package with pyproject.toml, src/erk_slots/ layout, and entry in uv workspace", "status": "in_progress", "pr": "#9273", "depends_on": [], "comment": null}, {"id": "1.2", "slug": "conditional-loading", "description": "Conditional plugin loading in cli.py: try import erk_slots, register slot group if available, replacing hardcoded import", "status": "in_progress", "pr": "#9273", "depends_on": ["1.1"], "comment": null}, {"id": "1.3", "slug": "move-slot-commands", "description": "Move slot CLI commands and common.py into erk_slots package, update internal imports", "status": "in_progress", "pr": "#9276", "depends_on": ["1.2"], "comment": null}, {"id": "1.4", "slug": "move-branch-checkout", "description": "Move branch checkout into slot checkout: create erk slot checkout in erk_slots with full slot-aware logic, remove branch checkout from core", "status": "pending", "pr": null, "depends_on": ["1.3"], "comment": null}, {"id": "1.5", "slug": "simplify-branch-create", "description": "Simplify branch create: remove allocate_slot_for_branch and update_slot_assignment_tip, create branch + direct worktree only", "status": "pending", "pr": null, "depends_on": ["1.4"], "comment": null}, {"id": "1.6", "slug": "decouple-config", "description": "Remove slot imports from config.py (DEFAULT_POOL_SIZE) \u2014 pool config internal to erk_slots", "status": "pending", "pr": null, "depends_on": ["1.5"], "comment": null}]}, {"number": 2, "suffix": "", "name": "Phase 2", "nodes": [{"id": "2.1", "slug": "move-navigation", "description": "Move navigation into erk_slots: slot up / slot down with slot-aware worktree allocation", "status": "pending", "pr": null, "depends_on": ["1.6"], "comment": null}, {"id": "2.2", "slug": "move-teleport", "description": "Move pr teleport into erk_slots: slot teleport with find_assignment_by_worktree and update_slot_assignment_tip", "status": "pending", "pr": null, "depends_on": ["2.1"], "comment": null}, {"id": "2.3", "slug": "move-wt-commands", "description": "Move wt create-from and wt delete into erk_slots: slot create-from and slot delete", "status": "pending", "pr": null, "depends_on": ["2.2"], "comment": null}, {"id": "2.4", "slug": "simplify-land", "description": "Simplify land: remove execute_unassign and pool state imports, rely on implicit pool reuse", "status": "pending", "pr": null, "depends_on": ["2.3"], "comment": null}, {"id": "2.5", "slug": "simplify-remaining", "description": "Simplify remaining: branch delete (remove unassign), branch/wt list (remove is_placeholder_branch), reconcile_pipeline, stack consolidate", "status": "pending", "pr": null, "depends_on": ["2.4"], "comment": null}, {"id": "2.6", "slug": "move-data-layer", "description": "Move worktree_pool.py and legacy_slot_naming health check into erk_slots package", "status": "pending", "pr": null, "depends_on": ["2.5"], "comment": null}, {"id": "2.7", "slug": "delete-slot-from-core", "description": "Delete erk.cli.commands.slot/ directory from core package entirely", "status": "pending", "pr": null, "depends_on": ["2.6"], "comment": null}]}], "summary": {"total_nodes": 13, "pending": 10, "planning": 0, "done": 0, "in_progress": 3, "blocked": 0, "skipped": 0}, "next_node": {"id": "1.4", "slug": "move-branch-checkout", "description": "Move branch checkout into slot checkout: create erk slot checkout in erk_slots with full slot-aware logic, remove branch checkout from core", "phase": "Phase 1", "status": "pending"}, "all_complete": false}, "validation": {"passed": true, "checks": [{"passed": true, "description": "Issue has erk-objective label"}, {"passed": true, "description": "Roadmap parses successfully"}, {"passed": true, "description": "Status/PR consistency"}, {"passed": true, "description": "No orphaned done statuses (done steps have PR references)"}, {"passed": true, "description": "Phase numbering is sequential"}, {"passed": true, "description": "objective-header has objective_comment_id"}, {"passed": true, "description": "PR references use '#' prefix"}, {"passed": true, "description": "Roadmap table in sync with YAML"}, {"passed": true, "description": "PR backlinks: all PR references have matching objective_issue backlinks"}]}, "marker_created": true, "warnings": []}
session.user:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/schrockn/.erk/repos/erk/worktrees/erk-slot-04/.claude/skills/objective
# Objective Skill
## Overview
Objectives are coordination documents for goals requiring multiple plans/PRs to complete. Unlike erk-plans (single executable implementations), objectives track progress across related work and capture lessons learned along the way.
**Scope range:**
- Small: Feature requiring 2-3 related PRs - Medium: Refactor spanning several plans - Large: Long-running strategic direction emitting many plans
## Objective vs Erk-Plan
| Aspect | Erk-Plan | Objective | | -------- | -------------------------------- | ----------------------------------- | | Purpose | Single executable implementation | Coordinate 2+ related plans/PRs | | Scope | One PR or tightly-coupled change | Multiple plans toward coherent goal | | Body | Machine-parseable metadata | Human-readable markdown | | Comments | Session context dumps | Action logs + lessons | | Label | `erk-plan` | `erk-objective` | | Tooling | `erk pr dispatch/implement` | Manual updates via comments |
## Key Design Principles
1. **Human-first** - Plain markdown, no machine-generated metadata 2. **Incremental capture** - Each action gets its own comment 3. **Lessons as first-class** - Every action comment includes lessons learned 4. **Clear roadmap** - Status visible at a glance in the body 5. **Body stays current via reconciliation** - After every PR landing, agents audit prose sections against what was actually implemented and correct stale information 6. **Steelthread-first** - Each phase starts with minimal vertical slice proving the concept works 7. **One PR per phase** - Each phase is sized for a coherent single PR 8. **Always shippable** - System remains functional after each merged PR 9. **Body is source of truth** - Body always contains complete current state; comments are the changelog 10. **Two-step for all changes** - Every addition (context, decisions, phases) gets a comment AND body update 11. **Context over code** - Provide references to patterns, not prescriptive implementations 12. **Session handoff ready** - Body should be self-contained for any session to pick up and implement
## Quick Reference
### Creating an Objective
```bash gh issue create --title "Objective: [Title]" --label "erk-objective" --body "$(cat <<'EOF' # Objective: [Title]
> [1-2 sentence summary]
## Goal
[What success looks like - concrete end state]
## Design Decisions
1. **[Decision name]**: [What was decided]
## Roadmap
### Phase 1: [Name] Steelthread (1 PR)
Minimal vertical slice proving the concept works.
| Node | Description | Status | PR | |------|-------------|--------|-----| | 1.1 | [Minimal infrastructure] | pending | | | 1.2 | [Wire into one command] | pending | |
**Test:** [End-to-end acceptance test for steelthread]
### Phase 2: Complete [Name] (1 PR)
Fill out remaining functionality.
| Node | Description | Status | PR | |------|-------------|--------|-----| | 2.1 | [Extend to remaining commands] | pending | | | 2.2 | [Full test coverage] | pending | |
**Test:** [Full acceptance criteria]
EOF )" ```
### Logging an Action
Post an action comment after completing work. See [format.md](references/format.md#action-comment-template) for full template.
```bash gh issue comment <issue-number> --body "$(cat <<'EOF' ## Action: [Brief title] **Date:** YYYY-MM-DD | **PR:** #123 | **Phase/Node:** 1.2 ### What Was Done ### Lessons Learned ### Roadmap Updates EOF )" ```
After posting, update the issue body (roadmap statuses, reconcile stale prose sections).
### Updating Node Details
Update node description, slug, or reason using `update-objective-node`:
```bash # Update description erk exec update-objective-node 8470 --node 1.3 --description "Revised description"
# Set slug erk exec update-objective-node 8470 --node 1.3 --slug "revised-slug"
# Skip with comment erk exec update-objective-node 8470 --node 1.3 --status skipped --comment "Superseded by new approach"
# Combine multiple updates erk exec update-objective-node 8470 --node 2.1 --description "New desc" --slug "new-slug" --status planning ```
### Adding Nodes
Add new nodes to an existing objective's roadmap:
```bash # Add to existing phase (auto-assigns next ID, e.g., 1.4) erk exec add-objective-node 8470 --phase 1 --description "Clean up dead code"
# With explicit slug and dependencies erk exec add-objective-node 8470 --phase 2 \ --description "Integration tests" \ --slug integration-tests \ --depends-on 2.1 --depends-on 2.2
# With comment for adding erk exec add-objective-node 8470 --phase 1 \ --description "Handle edge case" \ --comment "Discovered during re-evaluation" ```
### Updating Node Status
Always use the programmatic command — never manually edit YAML or prose tables:
```bash erk exec update-objective-node <issue> --node <id> --status <status> erk exec update-objective-node <issue> --node <id> --pr '#1234' ```
Then validate: `erk objective check <issue-number>`
### Viewing an Objective
View an objective's dependency graph, dependencies, and next node:
```bash # CLI erk objective view <issue-number>
# Slash command (in-session) /local:objective-view <issue-number> ```
### Spawning an Erk-Plan
To implement a specific roadmap node, create an erk-plan that references the objective:
```bash erk pr create --file plan.md --title "Implement [node description]" ```
## Workflow Summary
1. **Create objective** - When starting multi-plan work 2. **Inspect progress** - View dependency graph and next node 3. **Log actions** - After completing each significant piece of work 4. **Update body** - Keep roadmap status current, reconcile stale prose after each PR landing 5. **Spawn erk-plans** - For individual implementation nodes 6. **Close** - When goal achieved or abandoned (proactively ask when all nodes done)
## Resources
### references/
- `format.md` - Complete templates, examples, and update patterns - `workflow.md` - Creating objectives, spawning plans, steelthread structuring - `updating.md` - Quick reference for the two-step update workflow - `closing.md` - Closing triggers and procedures
session.assistant:
**Objective #9272: Extract Slot System into Plugin Package**
### Roadmap
| Phase | Node | Description | Status | PR | |-------|------|-------------|--------|-----| | 1 | 1.1 | Create erk-slots workspace package | in_progress | #9273 | | 1 | 1.2 | Conditional plugin loading in cli.py | in_progress | #9273 | | 1 | 1.3 | Move slot CLI commands + common.py | in_progress | #9276 | | 1 | **1.4** | **Move branch checkout into slot checkout** | **pending** | | | 1 | 1.5 | Simplify branch create | pending | | | 1 | 1.6 | Decouple config | pending | | | 2 | 2.1 | Move navigation (slot up/down) | pending | | | 2 | 2.2 | Move pr teleport into erk_slots | pending | | | 2 | 2.3 | Move wt create-from and wt delete | pending | | | 2 | 2.4 | Simplify land | pending | | | 2 | 2.5 | Simplify remaining commands | pending | | | 2 | 2.6 | Move data layer (worktree_pool.py) | pending | | | 2 | 2.7 | Delete slot directory from core | pending | |

tool_use.name: AskUserQuestion
tool_use.param: [{'question': 'Which node should I create a plan for?', 'header': 'Node', 'options': [{'la...[truncated 419 chars]...nfig.py (DEFAULT_POOL_SIZE) — pool config internal to erk_slots'}], 'multiSelect': False}]

session.tool_result: User has answered your questions: "Which node should I create a plan for?"="Node 1.4 (Recommended)". You can now continue with the user's answers in mind.
session.assistant: Creating plan for **Node 1.4: Move branch checkout into slot checkout**.
session.user:
# /erk:system:objective-plan-node
Inner skill for creating an implementation plan when the objective issue and node ID are already known. Called by the outer `/erk:objective-plan` command or directly by `plan_cmd.py` via Claude launch.
This skips interactive node selection (Steps 4-5 of the outer command) since the node is already determined.
## Usage
```bash /erk:system:objective-plan-node 3679 --node 2.1 ```
Both `<objective-number>` and `--node <node-id>` are required.
---
## Agent Instructions
### Step 1: Parse Arguments
Parse `9272 --node 1.4` to extract:
- **Objective number**: The numeric objective reference (required) - **Node ID**: The `--node` value (required)
If either is missing, STOP and report: "ERROR: Both objective number and --node are required. Usage: /erk:system:objective-plan-node <objective-number> --node <node-id>"
### Step 2: Create Objective Context Marker
Launch a Task agent to fetch objective data and create the context marker:
Use the Task tool with `subagent_type: "general-purpose"` and `model: "haiku"`:
**Task Prompt:**
``` Fetch and validate objective #<objective-number> and return a structured summary.
CRITICAL: Do NOT write scripts or code. Only use the Bash tool to run the erk CLI commands listed below.
Instructions: 1. Run: erk exec get-issue-body <objective-number> 2. Validate this is an objective: - Check for 'erk-objective' label - If 'erk-plan' label instead: return error "This is an erk-plan PR, not an objective" - If neither label: include warning but proceed 3. Create objective context marker: erk exec marker create --session-id "157bafef-00db-4abb-9d6e-c9a1f9cf51a2" --associated-objective <objective-number> objective-context 4. Run: erk objective check <objective-number> --json-output --allow-legacy 5. Format the JSON output from step 4 into the structured summary below. Do NOT write Python or any other scripts to parse the data — just read the JSON output directly and format it yourself.
OBJECTIVE: #<number> — <title> STATUS: <OPEN|CLOSED>
ROADMAP: | Node | Phase | Description | Status | | 1.1 | Phase 1 | <description> | done (PR #123) | | 1.2 | Phase 1 | <description> | pending | | 2.1 | Phase 2 | <description> | blocked |
PENDING_NODES: - 1.2: <description> - 3.1: <description>
RECOMMENDED: <node-id or "none">
WARNINGS: <any warnings about labels, roadmap format, etc., or "none">
Status mapping: - "pending" → "pending" - "done" → "done (PR #XXX)" - "in_progress" → "plan in progress (#XXX)" - "blocked" → "blocked" - "skipped" → "skipped"
Only include nodes with status "pending" in PENDING_NODES section. Use the "next_step" field from check output as RECOMMENDED. ```
Replace `<objective-number>` with the parsed objective number.
### Step 3: Verify Objective Context Marker
```bash erk exec marker read --session-id "157bafef-00db-4abb-9d6e-c9a1f9cf51a2" objective-context ```
If this returns a value matching the objective issue number, proceed. If it fails or returns wrong value, STOP and report: "ERROR: objective-context marker not created. Re-run the marker command manually: erk exec marker create --session-id '157bafef-00db-4abb-9d6e-c9a1f9cf51a2' --associated-objective <issue-number> objective-context"
### Step 4: Create Roadmap Node Marker and Mark as Planning
Create the roadmap-step marker for the known node:
```bash erk exec marker create --session-id "157bafef-00db-4abb-9d6e-c9a1f9cf51a2" \ --content "<node-id>" roadmap-step ```
Replace `<node-id>` with the node ID from arguments.
Then mark the node as `planning` in the objective's roadmap (best-effort — may already be marked by the CLI):
```bash erk exec update-objective-node <objective-number> --node <node-id> --status planning ```
If this fails, continue with planning — the CLI may have already marked it.
### Step 5: Load Objective Skill
Load the `objective` skill for format templates and guidance.
### Step 6: Gather Context
Before entering plan mode, gather relevant context:
1. **Objective context:** Goal, design decisions, implementation context 2. **Node context:** What the specific node requires 3. **Prior work:** Look at completed nodes and their PRs for patterns
Use this context to inform the plan.
### Step 7: Enter Plan Mode
Enter plan mode to create the implementation plan:
1. Use the EnterPlanMode tool 2. Focus the plan on the specific node selected 3. Reference the parent objective in the plan
**Plan should include:**
- Reference to objective: `Part of Objective #<number>, Node <node-id>` - Clear goal for this specific node - Implementation phases (typically 1-3 for a single node) - Files to modify - Test requirements
### Step 8: Save Plan as PR (Always Save)
After the plan is approved in plan mode, **always save as a PR** — do not offer direct implementation. When the `exit-plan-mode-hook` fires, proceed directly with `/erk:plan-save`. Objective node plans require PR tracking for the objective's roadmap status to work correctly.
The objective-context marker created in Step 2 is automatically read by `/erk:plan-save`. Simply run `/erk:plan-save` and it will link the plan to the objective.
**If the marker was not created (fallback):** Create it manually before saving:
```bash erk exec marker create --session-id "157bafef-00db-4abb-9d6e-c9a1f9cf51a2" --associated-objective <objective-number> objective-context ```
Then run `/erk:plan-save`.
### Step 9: Verify Objective Link
After saving, the JSON output includes `objective_issue`. Check that it matches the expected objective number.
If verification is needed:
```bash erk exec get-plan-metadata <new-plan-number> objective_issue ```
Check that `value` matches the expected objective number.
---
## Output Format
- **Start:** "Planning node <node-id> of objective #<number>..." - **After context:** Display node description and gathered context - **In plan mode:** Show plan content - **End:** Always proceed with `/erk:plan-save` — do not offer direct implementation
---
## Error Cases
| Scenario | Action | | --------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------- | | Missing objective number or node ID | Report error with usage instructions | | Objective not found | Report error and exit | | Reference is erk-plan | Redirect to `/erk:plan-implement` | | Node not found in roadmap | Report error and list available nodes | | Marker creation fails | Report error with manual command | | Verification fails (no objective_issue) | `/erk:plan-save` handles automatically; follow remediation steps |
---
## Important Notes
- **This is an inner skill** — it expects the node to be pre-selected, not interactive - **Best-effort marking** — the `planning` status update may already be done by the CLI before Claude launches - **Objective context matters:** Read the full objective for design decisions and lessons learned - **One node at a time:** Each plan should focus on a single roadmap node - **Link back:** Always reference the parent objective in the plan
session.assistant:
Planning node 1.4 of objective #9272...
Let me gather the objective context and set up markers.
Markers set up. Now let me gather context about the current codebase state to inform the plan.

tool_use.name: Agent
tool_use.param: FIRST check docs/learned/index.md for existing documentation on this topic. Read relevant ...[truncated 1155 chars]...y in erk_slots package - The objective's design decisions and any relevant lessons learned

tool_use.param: FIRST check docs/learned/index.md for existing documentation on this topic. Read relevant ...[truncated 1142 chars]...mmon.py module contents - What functions from worktree_pool.py are used by branch checkout

tool_use.name: Agent
tool_use.param: FIRST check docs/learned/index.md for existing documentation on this topic. Read relevant ...[truncated 1117 chars]...age imports or coupling patterns - The branch structure (are we stacking on top of #9276?)

session.tool_result:
Perfect. Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a detailed summary report:
## Summary Report: Objective #9272 Node 1.4 - Understanding the Branch Checkout Migration
Based on my analysis of the codebase, here's a comprehensive report on the current implementation and what needs to be migrated:
---
### Current State Overview
**Objective Goal**: Move `erk branch checkout` command into erk-slots package as `erk slot checkout` with full slot-aware logic, removing the branch checkout implementation from core.
**Status**: - Nodes 1.1–1.2: Complete (PR #9273) - erk-slots package created with conditional loading - Node 1.3: In Progress (PR #9276) - Slot commands and common.py moved to erk-slots - Node 1.4: Planning - Branch checkout migration
---
### 1. Current `erk branch checkout` Implementation
**Location**: `/Users/schrockn/.erk/repos/erk/worktrees/erk-slot-04/src/erk/cli/commands/branch/checkout_cmd.py` (707 lines)
**Key Functions**:
| Function | Lines | Purpose | |----------|-------|---------| | `branch_checkout()` | 416-462 | Click command entry point with `@alias("co")` | | `_branch_checkout_impl()` | 465-707 | Main implementation logic (checks args, resolves branch, finds/creates worktree) | | `try_switch_root_worktree()` | 41-77 | "Takeover" behavior: switches clean root to trunk instead of creating new slot |
... [243 lines omitted] ...
| `script_error_handler()` | 47-75 | Context manager for --script error handling | - `test_slot_checkout_pool_full_no_force_fails()` - Non-TTY pool-full error

Perfect. Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me provide a complete report:
## Analysis Report: Prior Node Implementation Patterns (Objective #9272 Nodes 1.1-1.3)
### Summary
I've reviewed PR #9273 (nodes 1.1-1.2) and PR #9276 (nodes 1.3-1.4), examined the current codebase structure, and analyzed the implementation patterns. You're currently on branch `plnd/O9272-move-slot-cmds-check-03-20-1504` which has both nodes 1.1-1.3 already implemented.
---
### Key Implementation Patterns from Nodes 1.1-1.3
#### **Node 1.1-1.2: Package Creation + Conditional Loading**
**What was created:** - New workspace package at `packages/erk-slots/` with standard layout: - `pyproject.toml` declaring `erk-slots` as a workspace member - Dependency on `erk-shared` only (not on core erk to avoid circular deps) - Version pinned to `0.9.11` - Initial `erk_slots/__init__.py` re-exported `slot_group` from `erk.cli.commands.slot` (thin wrapper) - Registered in root `pyproject.toml`: - Added to `[tool.uv.workspace]` members - Added to `[tool.uv.sources]` with `{ workspace = true }` - Added `erk_slots` to `[tool.ruff.lint.isort]` known-first-party - Added test paths to pytest config - Conditional loading in `cli.py` using simple `try/except ImportError` pattern (not `is_learned_docs_available`)
**File**: `/Users/schrockn/.erk/repos/erk/worktrees/erk-slot-04/packages/erk-slots/pyproject.toml`
#### **Node 1.3: Move Files + Reorganize Package**
... [167 lines omitted] ...

Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me create a comprehensive report:
## Summary: erk-slots Package Structure and Gateway/Service Layer
### 1. Complete erk-slots Package Structure
**Location**: `/Users/schrockn/.erk/repos/erk/worktrees/erk-slot-04/packages/erk-slots/`
**Package Metadata**: - **pyproject.toml**: Version 0.9.11, requires Python 3.11+, depends on `click>=8.1.7` and `erk-shared` - **Build**: Uses hatchling, packages `src/erk_slots`
**Source Files** (`src/erk_slots/`): 1. **`__init__.py`** (817B) - Defines the `slot_group()` Click command group and registers all slot commands: - `slot_assign` - `slot_checkout` - `slot_init_pool` - `slot_list` - `slot_repair` - `slot_unassign`
2. **`common.py`** (754 lines, 24.5K) - Core utilities and the unified allocation algorithm: - `extract_slot_number()` / `generate_slot_name()` - Slot naming helpers - `get_placeholder_branch_name()` / `is_placeholder_branch()` - Placeholder branch management - `get_pool_size()` - Read pool size from config or use default (4 slots) - `find_next_available_slot()` - Fast path: find unassigned slot not on disk - `find_inactive_slot()` - Reuse path: find worktree without staged/modified files - `sync_pool_assignments()` - Reconcile pool.json with actual git state (handles manual `git checkout` / `gt create`) - `find_branch_assignment()` / `find_assignment_by_worktree()` - Lookup functions - `find_oldest_assignment()` - For LRU eviction
... [248 lines omitted] ...
**Lazy State Correction**: If user runs `git checkout` manually in a slot, pool silently corrects state on next allocation (no error)
session.assistant: Now let me read the critical files to deepen my understanding before designing the plan.
session.tool_result:
1→"""Checkout command - find and switch to a worktree by branch name.""" 2→ 3→import contextlib 4→import sys 5→from pathlib import Path 6→ 7→import click 8→ 9→from erk.cli.activation import ( 10→ activation_config_activate_only, 11→ ensure_worktree_activate_script, 12→ print_activation_instructions, 13→ render_activation_script, 14→) 15→from erk.cli.commands.checkout_helpers import ( 16→ display_sync_status, 17→ navigate_to_worktree, 18→ script_error_handler, 19→) 20→from erk.cli.commands.completions import complete_branch_names 21→from erk.cli.commands.pr.dispatch_helpers import sync_branch_to_sha 22→from erk.cli.core import discover_repo_context 23→from erk.cli.github_parsing import parse_issue_identifier 24→from erk.cli.graphite import find_worktrees_containing_branch 25→from erk.cli.help_formatter import CommandWithHiddenOptions, script_option 26→from erk.core.context import ErkContext 27→from erk.core.repo_discovery import RepoContext, ensure_erk_metadata_dir 28→from erk.core.worktree_utils import compute_relative_path_in_worktree 29→from erk_shared.cli_alias import alias 30→from erk_shared.gateway.git.abc import WorktreeInfo
... [677 lines omitted] ...
36→ PlanValidationFailed, 409→ f"Warning: Rebase had conflicts. Worktree created but needs manual rebase.
" 455→ handler = script_error_handler(ctx) if script else contextlib.nullcontext() 472→ """Implementation body for branch_checkout — separated for error wrapping.""" 476→ "Error: Cannot specify both BRANCH and --for-plan.
" 482→ user_output("Error: Must provide BRANCH argument or --for-plan option.") 500→ raise click.ClickException(f"PR #{pr_number} not found") 504→ if isinstance(plan_result, PlanValidationFailed): 505→ user_output(f"Error: {plan_result.message}") 511→ for warning in setup.warnings: 512→ user_output(click.style("Warning: ", fg="yellow") + warning) 521→ user_output("Error: Could not detect trunk branch.") 576→ f"Error: Branch '{branch}' does not exist.
" 694→ f"Error: Internal state mismatch. Branch '{branch}' was allocated "

1→"""Slot checkout command - checkout a branch into a pool slot.""" 2→ 3→import click 4→ 5→from erk.cli.core import discover_repo_context 6→from erk.core.context import ErkContext 7→from erk.core.repo_discovery import ensure_erk_metadata_dir 8→from erk.core.worktree_pool import load_pool_state 9→from erk_shared.output.output import user_output 10→from erk_slots.common import ( 11→ allocate_slot_for_branch, 12→ find_current_slot_assignment, 13→ update_slot_assignment_tip, 14→) 15→ 16→ 17→@click.command("checkout") 18→@click.argument("branch", metavar="BRANCH") 19→@click.option( 20→ "--new-slot", 21→ is_flag=True, 22→ help="Allocate a new slot instead of stacking in place", 23→) 24→@click.option( 25→ "-f", 26→ "--force", 27→ is_flag=True, 28→ help="Auto-unassign oldest branch if pool is full", 29→) 30→@click.pass_obj
... [80 lines omitted] ...
62→ f"Error: Branch '{branch}' does not exist.\n"

1→"""Checkout navigation helpers - shared across checkout commands. 2→ 3→This module is separate from navigation_helpers.py to avoid circular imports. 4→navigation_helpers imports from wt.create_cmd, which triggers wt/__init__.py, 5→which imports wt.checkout_cmd. By having checkout-specific helpers here, 6→we break that cycle. 7→""" 8→ 9→import contextlib 10→import logging 11→import sys 12→from collections.abc import Generator, Sequence 13→from pathlib import Path 14→ 15→import click 16→ 17→from erk.cli.activation import ( 18→ ensure_worktree_activate_script, 19→ render_activation_script, 20→ render_error_script, 21→) 22→from erk.cli.core import worktree_path_for 23→from erk.core.context import ErkContext 24→from erk.core.repo_discovery import RepoContext 25→from erk_shared.output.output import user_output 26→from erk_slots.common import allocate_slot_for_branch 27→ 28→logger = logging.getLogger(__name__) 29→ 30→
... [299 lines omitted] ...
31→def _write_error_script_and_exit(ctx: ErkContext) -> None: 32→ """Write a minimal error script and output its path, then exit 1. 34→ Used by ``script_error_handler`` to ensure ``source "$(erk br co --script)"`` 35→ always receives a valid file path — even on failure — so the shell never 39→ render_error_script(), 41→ comment="checkout error", 48→def script_error_handler(ctx: ErkContext) -> Generator[None]: 49→ """Catch errors in --script mode and emit an error script instead of empty stdout. 51→ Wraps the checkout body so that any error path produces a valid script 54→ ``source: no such file or directory:`` error. 56→ Exception handling: 58→ - ``SystemExit(non-zero)``: Error already printed via ``user_output()``; 59→ write error script and exit 1. 60→ - ``click.ClickException``: Format the error ourselves (Click's handler 61→ won't run since we catch first), write error script, exit 1. 62→ - ``RuntimeError``: Print to stderr, write error script, exit 1. 69→ _write_error_script_and_exit(ctx) 70→ except click.ClickException as exc: 71→ user_output(f"Error: {exc.format_message()}") 72→ _write_error_script_and_exit(ctx) 73→ except RuntimeError as exc: 74→ user_output(f"Error: {exc}") 75→ _write_error_script_and_exit(ctx) 116→ - Diverged: Warning with instructions 129→ # get_ahead_behind raises RuntimeError on subprocess failure (e.g., network issues). 132→ except RuntimeError: 133→ logger.debug("Failed to get ahead/behind for %s on %s", branch, worktree_path) 143→ # Diverged - most important case, needs warning 144→ warning = click.style("⚠ Local has diverged from origin:", fg="yellow") 146→ user_output(f" {warning} {styled_sync}")

1→"""Worktree pool slot management plugin for erk.""" 2→ 3→import click 4→ 5→from erk_shared.cli_alias import register_with_aliases 6→from erk_shared.cli_group import ErkCommandGroup 7→from erk_slots.assign_cmd import slot_assign 8→from erk_slots.checkout_cmd import slot_checkout 9→from erk_slots.init_pool_cmd import slot_init_pool 10→from erk_slots.list_cmd import slot_list 11→from erk_slots.repair_cmd import slot_repair 12→from erk_slots.unassign_cmd import slot_unassign 13→ 14→ 15→@click.group("slot", cls=ErkCommandGroup, grouped=False) 16→def slot_group() -> None: 17→ """Manage worktree pool slots.""" 18→ pass 19→ 20→ 21→slot_group.add_command(slot_assign) 22→slot_group.add_command(slot_checkout) 23→slot_group.add_command(slot_init_pool) 24→slot_group.add_command(slot_repair) 25→slot_group.add_command(slot_unassign) 26→register_with_aliases(slot_group, slot_list) 27→

1→"""Branch management commands.""" 2→ 3→import click 4→ 5→from erk.cli.commands.branch.checkout_cmd import branch_checkout 6→from erk.cli.commands.branch.create_cmd import branch_create 7→from erk.cli.commands.branch.delete_cmd import branch_delete 8→from erk.cli.commands.branch.list_cmd import branch_list 9→from erk_shared.cli_alias import alias, register_with_aliases 10→from erk_shared.cli_group import ErkCommandGroup 11→ 12→ 13→@alias("br") 14→@click.group("branch", cls=ErkCommandGroup, grouped=False) 15→def branch_group() -> None: 16→ """Manage branches.""" 17→ pass 18→ 19→ 20→# Register subcommands 21→branch_group.add_command(branch_create) 22→branch_group.add_command(branch_delete) 23→register_with_aliases(branch_group, branch_checkout) 24→register_with_aliases(branch_group, branch_list) 25→

tool_use.name: Bash
tool_use.param: Get objective #9272 body for design decisions

session.tool_result:
{"success": true, "issue_number": 9272, "title": "Objective: Extract Slot System into Plugin Package", "body": "
objective-header ```yaml created_at: '2026-03-20T14:20:11.429236+00:00' created_by: schrockn objective_comment_id: 4100113739 slug: extract-slots-plugin-package ```
objective-roadmap ```yaml schema_version: '4' nodes: - id: '1.1' slug: create-slots-package description: Create packages/erk-slots/ workspace package with pyproject.toml, src/erk_slots/ layout, and entry in uv workspace status: done pr: '#9273' - id: '1.2' slug: conditional-loading description: 'Conditional plugin loading in cli.py: importlib.util.find_spec(''erk_slots'') check to register slot group if available, replacing hardcoded import' status: in_progress pr: '#9273' - id: '1.3' slug: move-slot-commands description: Move slot CLI commands and common.py into erk_slots package, update internal imports status: in_progress pr: '#9276' - id: '1.4' slug: move-branch-checkout description: 'Move branch checkout into slot checkout: create erk slot checkout in erk_slots with full slot-aware logic, remove branch checkout from core' status: planning pr: null - id: '1.5' slug: simplify-branch-create description: 'Simplify branch create: remove allocate_slot_for_branch and update_slot_assignment_tip, create branch + direct worktree only' status: pending pr: null - id: '1.6' slug: decouple-config description: Remove slot imports from config.py (DEFAULT_POOL_SIZE) — pool config internal to erk_slots status: pending pr: null - id: '2.1' slug: move-navigation description: 'Move navigation into erk_slots: slot up / slot down with slot-aware worktree allocation' status: pending pr: null - id: '2.2' slug: move-teleport description: 'Move pr teleport into erk_slots: slot teleport with find_assignment_by_worktree and update_slot_assignment_tip' status: pending pr: null - id: '2.3' slug: move-wt-commands description: 'Move wt create-from and wt delete into erk_slots: slot create-from and slot delete' status: pending pr: null - id: '2.4' slug: simplify-land description: 'Simplify land: remove execute_unassign and pool state imports, rely on implicit pool reuse' status: pending pr: null - id: '2.5' slug: simplify-remaining description: 'Simplify remaining: branch delete (remove unassign), branch/wt list (remove is_placeholder_branch), reconcile_pipeline, stack consolidate' status: pending pr: null - id: '2.6' slug: move-data-layer description: Move worktree_pool.py and legacy_slot_naming health check into erk_slots package status: pending pr: null - id: '2.7' slug: delete-slot-from-core description: Delete erk.cli.commands.slot/ directory from core package entirely status: pending pr: null ```
", "state": "OPEN", "labels": ["erk-objective"], "url": "https://github.com/dagster-io/erk/issues/9272"}

1→--- 2→title: Checkout Three-Path Logic 3→category: cli 4→read_when: 5→ - "modifying erk br co" 6→ - "working with --for-plan flag" 7→ - "changing slot allocation behavior" 8→last_audited: "2026-03-05 00:00 PT" 9→audit_result: clean 10→--- 11→ 12→# Checkout Three-Path Logic 13→ 14→`erk br co` uses a three-path decision tree when `--for-plan` is involved, implemented in `branch/checkout_cmd.py:614-717`. 15→ 16→## Decision Tree 17→ 18→### Path 1: Stack in Place (`current_assignment is not None`) 19→ 20→The user is in a slot-assigned worktree and the branch has a current assignment. 21→ 22→- Updates assignment to new tip 23→- Calls `_rebase_and_track_for_plan()` if setup exists 24→- Calls `_setup_impl_for_plan()` if setup exists 25→- Calls `_perform_checkout()` with `force_script_activation=True` 26→- Returns early 27→ 28→### Path 2: Checkout in Current Worktree (`setup is not None and not new_slot`) 29→ 30→The user passed `--for-plan` (so `setup is not None`) but did NOT pass `--new-slot`.
... [30 lines omitted] ...
